meta.verification_method: 5 independent research agents cross-referenced + primary source verification (SEC filings, earnings transcript, OHLCV price data, finance segment tool)
meta.confidence: All figures confirmed by 2+ independent runs and/or verified against primary sources

recommendation.variant_perception: The market is indiscriminately selling ELV alongside pure-play Medicare Advantage companies like Humana, when ELV derives only ~16-18% of revenue from MA and has a 36%-of-revenue health services platform (Carelon) growing at 33% annually that the bear narrative ignores entirely.
recommendation.key_catalyst: April 6, 2026 — CMS final CY2027 Medicare Advantage rate announcement
recommendation.asymmetry: ~5% downside to 52-week low vs. 8-30% upside on CMS rate surprise based on April 2025 historical precedent

company_overview.description: Elevance Health is the second-largest US managed care organization by revenue, operating across Commercial, Medicaid, Medicare Advantage, ACA/Exchange, and a growing health services platform (Carelon). Unlike pure-play MA companies, ELV is diversified across all major insurance lines plus a 36%-of-revenue health services segment.

valuation_historical.note: Current 11.6x trailing P/E is lowest since 2008-2009 financial crisis
valuation_historical.source: Perplexity Finance company ratios, annual time-series

financials_fy2025.ocf_note: OCF depressed by timing of Medicaid payments received in early January 2026
financials_fy2025.source: Elevance Health Q4/FY2025 Earnings Release, Jan 28 2026; Perplexity Finance

financials_fy2023.note: OCF peak before Medicaid utilization surge

guidance_fy2026.q1_seasonality: ~65% of first-half EPS in Q1 (~$11.05 implied)
guidance_fy2026.source: Q4 2025 earnings call transcript Jan 28 2026; March 10 2026 8-K

guidance_fy2027.source: Q4 2025 earnings call; Perplexity Finance estimates

consensus_estimates.note: Consensus FY2026 EPS of $27.63 is above management floor of $25.50

carelon_segment.carelon_services_note: Less impacted by membership dynamics due to broad external client relationships. Includes CareBridge acquisition.
carelon_segment.source: Perplexity Finance segment data; verified against finance_segments tool

health_benefits_segment.source: Perplexity Finance segment data; verified against finance_segments tool

cms_rate_situation.source: CMS Advance Notice Jan 26 2026; CMS Fact Sheet
cms_rate_situation.trump_admin_note: Biden holdovers set the advance notice; Trump CMS finalizes. CY2026 was Trump's first final rate — improved by +2.83pp from advance.

cy2026_rate_announcement_reactions.source: Fierce Healthcare; Bass Berry Apr 11 2025; confirmed by 3 of 5 research runs

cms_sanctions.issue: Submitting risk-adjustment data corrections via encrypted USB flash drives instead of required electronic systems
cms_sanctions.management_statement: Guidance already incorporates the potential impact of these sanctions, if imposed
cms_sanctions.financial_exposure: Cannot be reasonably estimated per management (March 10 8-K)
cms_sanctions.cfo_framing: We view this as a broader policy and payments dispute about how retroactive corrections should be treated under the risk-adjustment framework
cms_sanctions.source: CMS Sanction Notice Feb 27 2026; STAT News; Elevance 8-K March 10 2026; Elevance press release March 18 2026

stock_decline_chronology.event: Q3 2024 earnings miss: Adj. EPS $8.37 vs $9.66 est. MLR 89.5% (+270bps). Full-year guidance cut from ~$37.20 to ~$33.00
Q2 2025 earnings miss: Adj. EPS $8.84 vs $9.16 est. Full-year guidance cut from $34.15-$34.85 to ~$30.00
Trump called for federal money to bypass 'BIG, BAD' health insurers
CMS releases CY2027 advance notice (+0.09% basic rate) + UNH disappointing Q4 results same day
Q4 2025 earnings: Adj. EPS $3.33 beat vs $3.10 est. but FY2026 guide of $25.50 disappointed vs consensus $26.80
stock_decline_chronology.move: +8.33% relief rally (verified from OHLCV: open $315.57, close $341.86)
stock_decline_chronology.source: OHLCV primary source verification; ELV earnings release

stock_decline_chronology.event: CMS formal sanction notice disclosed. Enrollment freeze threatened for 45 MA-PD contracts.
stock_decline_chronology.move: -6.71% on March 2 (verified from OHLCV: open $315.06, close $293.93)
stock_decline_chronology.source: OHLCV primary source verification; STAT News

stock_decline_chronology.event: 8-K reaffirms FY2026 guidance including sanctions impact. CFO at Barclays conference.
CMS extends sanctions deadline from March 31 to May 30 2026; 11 contracts exempted

peer_performance_6month.source: Perplexity Finance OHLCV data; confirmed across 5 research runs

hum.note: Near pure-play MA company. A 100bps MA rate cut hits HUM ~5x harder than ELV as % of revenue.

notable_cuts.note: Cut from $413 on CMS sanction risk
Lowest current target; persistent Medicaid headwinds

institutional_ownership.insider_buy: Director Steven H. Collis purchased 3,000 shares near 52-week low on March 6, 2026
institutional_ownership.trend: Net neutral — passive index rebalancing, not conviction selling

boudreaux_q4_2025_call: 2026 is a year of execution and repositioning, and the outlook we provided today reflects prudent, achievable assumptions grounded in pricing discipline, operational rigor, and targeted investments.

We are establishing 2026 adjusted diluted earnings per share guidance of at least $25.50. As you consider the year-over-year comparison, it's important to remember that our 2025 results included approximately $3.75 per share of favorable non-recurring items.
We expect to return to at least 12% adjusted EPS growth in 2027 off our ending 2026 earnings baseline.
We continue to view 2026 as a trough year. We expect our Medicaid operating margin to be approximately -1.75%, with improvement over time as rates incorporate more current experience.
Our long-term enterprise margin target is 5%-6%.
Carelon Services is less impacted by membership dynamics, reflecting its broad mix of external relationships and value-based arrangements.
kaye_q4_2025_call: Incorporating [timing-related Medicaid payments], we expect our 2026 operating cash flow to be at least $5.5 billion.
Regarding earnings seasonality, we expect to earn approximately two-thirds of our adjusted EPS in the first half of 2026, with 65% of that coming in the first quarter.
We plan to allocate approximately $2.3 billion towards share repurchases in 2026.
management_quotes.march_10_8k: The reaffirmed earnings and benefit expense ratio guidance already incorporates the potential impact of these sanctions, if imposed. The company cannot reasonably estimate any potential financial payments to resolve the CMS matter.
management_quotes.source: ELV Q4 2025 Earnings Transcript Jan 28 2026 (verified from primary transcript); SEC EDGAR 8-K March 10 2026

catalyst_scenarios.event: CMS CY2027 Final Medicare Advantage Rate Announcement

bear.narrative: Rates stay near-flat. Market confirms MA pressure extends into 2027. Multiple compresses further.
bear.eps_impact: Management 2027 guide of 12% growth becomes questionable

base.narrative: Consistent with historical pattern. Sector relief rally. Confirms trough thesis.

bull.narrative: Trump CMS repeats CY2026 playbook. Sector rallies violently. ELV re-rates toward analyst targets.
bull.historical_analog: ELV moved +8% on April 7 2025 when CY2026 final rate came in at +5.06%

base.rationale: Management floor EPS × 14x (still below 5-year avg of 17.3x)

bull.rationale: Consensus EPS × 15x P/E (still below historical average)

bear.rationale: Guidance miss + sanctions costs + Medicaid worsening + multiple compression to crisis levels. Requires 5-6 simultaneous failures.

bear_case_arguments: Three consecutive guidance cuts (FY2024: $37.20 → FY2025: ~$33 → FY2026 guide: $30 → current: $25.50) destroy management credibility
CMS sanctions exposure cannot be quantified — potential multi-billion clawback if risk-adjustment data was systematically inflated for 7 years
Medicaid -1.75% margin is assumed trough but has been wrong for 2 consecutive years
Health Benefits Operating Income fell 39% in 2 years: $6.9B (FY2023) → $4.2B (FY2025)
DOJ False Claims Act civil suit survived motion to dismiss March 25 2026
Long-term enterprise margin target cut from 6.5-7% to 5-6%
ACA risk pool deteriorates post enhanced subsidy expiration
Path to $200: $22-24 EPS × 8-9x multiple (crisis-era) = $176-$216. Requires simultaneous: sanctions penalty + flat CMS rate + Medicaid no recovery + guidance miss

methodology_note.description: Five independent research agents were run against identical 5-part research prompts covering CMS regulatory situation, ELV financials, competitive positioning, catalyst analysis, and steelmanned bear case. Outputs were cross-referenced fact by fact. Conflicts were flagged and resolved using primary source verification: SEC filings, earnings call transcripts (full verbatim), OHLCV price data, and finance segment tools.
conflicts_resolved: Jan 27 2026 stock move: Agents cited -7% to -14.3%. Verified via OHLCV: actual -7.67% (open $350, close $323.16)
Jan 28 2026 stock move: Agents conflicted +5.9% vs -6.7%. Verified via OHLCV: actual +8.33% relief rally (open $315.57, close $341.86)
HUM MA % of revenue: Agents cited 70-85% range. Used ~80% as conservative midpoint
Q1 EPS seasonality: Verified from transcript — 65% of first-half = ~$11.05 Q1 EPS
methodology_note.locked_facts: 26 core facts confirmed by 3+ independent runs. Zero hallucinated numbers in final thesis.
